metadata.summary: Go standard layout toolchain: CLI tools, linters, DB access generators, live-reload, migration runners, CI services, and testing infrastructure paired with the standard directory structure.

content.title: Go Standard Layout — Toolchain Reference
content.summary: The Go standard layout is toolchain-agnostic, but each slot in the layout (cmd/, internal/handler, internal/repository, migrations/) maps to a preferred set of tools. This reference pairs tools to layout slots so agents select the right binary for the job rather than inventing combinations.

why.p: Agents working on Go projects frequently mix incompatible tool choices: using GORM in a repo layer that was designed for sqlc, adding a zerolog init() alongside a pre-existing slog setup, or wiring cobra and urfave/cli in the same cmd/. A layout-aware toolchain reference prevents these collisions by mapping each tool to its layout slot and flagging common combination mistakes.

list.item: Starting a new Go service and selecting tools for each layout layer.
Onboarding an agent to an existing Go service to prevent it from introducing incompatible tools.
Code review where the agent must verify that tools are used in the correct layer.
Projects with a locked-in toolchain documented in go-stack.md — use the project-specific record instead.
Non-Go projects — toolchain selections are Go-specific.

section.name: cli-tools
section.p: Core CLI tools paired to their layout slot:

list.item: go — build, test, fmt, vet, mod. https://go.dev
golangci-lint — aggregator linter (errcheck, govet, staticcheck, revive, depguard). Commit .golangci.yml; run in CI on every PR. https://golangci-lint.run
depguard (golangci) — forbid imports across package boundaries. Key for enforcing handler/service/repository dependency direction. https://github.com/OpenPeeDeeP/depguard
gosec — security linter (hardcoded creds, weak crypto). Run in CI; alerts land in SARIF. https://github.com/securego/gosec
goimports / gofumpt — import ordering and stricter formatting (replaces gofmt). https://github.com/mvdan/gofumpt
gomod-outdated / go-mod-upgrade — track stale dependencies. https://github.com/psampaz/go-mod-outdated
mockgen (uber-go/mock) — generate mocks against interfaces. Pair with consumer-side interface placement. https://github.com/uber-go/mock
sqlc / gorm gen / ent — generate typed DB access from SQL schema. sqlc for strict SQL control; gorm gen or ent for ORM-first. https://sqlc.dev
air / wgo — live-reload during dev (inner loop). https://github.com/cosmtrek/air
delve (dlv) — debugger. https://github.com/go-delve/delve
pprof — CPU, alloc, goroutine, mutex profiling. Bundled with Go.
goreleaser — cross-compile and release artifacts from cmd/. https://goreleaser.com
cobra / urfave/cli — CLI scaffolding for cmd/* binaries. Pick one per repo. https://cobra.dev
koanf / viper — config loading from file/env/flag for internal/config. https://github.com/knadh/koanf
migrate / goose / atlas — schema migration runners driven by cmd/migrate. https://github.com/golang-migrate/migrate

section.name: ci-services
section.p: CI and observability services that are agent-friendly for Go standard layout projects:

list.item: GitHub Actions with setup-go — standard CI for go test ./... and golangci-lint run. SaaS, agent-friendly.
SonarCloud / DeepSource / Codacy — architecture rules and smell detection per PR. SaaS, agent-friendly.
Renovate / Dependabot — auto-PR Go module bumps; agents triage. SaaS, agent-friendly.
Sentry / Honeycomb / Datadog APM — native Go SDKs; instrument internal/middleware. SaaS, agent-friendly.
OpenTelemetry Go SDK — one init in cmd/api/main.go, instrumentation across internal/*. OSS, agent-friendly.
Air / Tilt / Skaffold — inner dev loop with hot reload. OSS, agent-friendly.
testcontainers-go — real Postgres/Redis in tests; first-class for repository implementations. OSS, agent-friendly.

section.name: agent-selection-model
section.p: Agent model selection by task type within Go standard layout work:

list.item: Database schema design and normalization — Opus. Requires architectural decisions and complex trade-offs.
Implement Go concurrency patterns — Sonnet. Coding with existing patterns, medium complexity.
Write database migration scripts — Haiku. Mechanical task using templates.
Review error handling implementation — Sonnet. Code review and refactoring.
Profile and optimize slow queries — Opus. Novel optimization problem, deep analysis.
Setup Redis caching layer — Sonnet. Medium complexity implementation task.

rule.statement: Pick exactly one CLI framework per repo for cmd/* binaries (cobra OR urfave/cli). Do not mix them across cmd/ subdirectories.
rule.rationale: Mixed CLI frameworks in the same module produce inconsistent UX and make it impossible to share flag definitions or version output between binaries.

rule.statement: Pick exactly one config library per repo (koanf OR viper). Initialize it once in internal/config and pass the config struct to constructors; do not call the config library from internal/service or internal/handler.
rule.rationale: Config libraries that are called from service or handler code create hidden coupling to environment variables that is invisible in unit tests.

rule.statement: Pick exactly one migration tool per repo (migrate, goose, or atlas). Wire it to a dedicated cmd/migrate binary. Do not run migrations as a side effect of cmd/api startup.
rule.rationale: Running migrations at API startup couples the API service to schema change authority; this causes race conditions in horizontal scaling and prevents independent migration dry-runs.

rule.statement: testcontainers-go MUST be used for repository-layer integration tests. Do not mock the database in repository tests — test against the real engine.
rule.rationale: In-memory fakes miss SQL dialect differences, constraint violations, and transaction semantics that only appear against the real engine. testcontainers-go starts the DB in Docker with no external setup.

section.name: quick-reference-prompts
section.p: Prompt templates for common Go layout toolchain tasks:
section.prompt:
Generate go standard layout implementation for [language].
Requirements: - Focus: Project layout conventions, best practices - Features: - [Feature 1] - [Feature 2] - [Feature 3] - Error handling: [strategy] - Testing: [unit/integration]
Include: 1. Core implementation 2. Error handling 3. Logging 4. Tests 5. Documentation 6. Usage examples

Review my go standard layout implementation for best practices.
Code: [paste implementation]
Review for: 1. Code quality 2. Error handling 3. Security issues 4. Performance concerns 5. Testing gaps 6. Documentation completeness
Provide specific recommendations with improved code.

Debug go standard layout issue: Error: [message] Code: [paste] Context: [details]
Diagnose and provide fix.
